posts.text: so in your opinion iran should just purchase hamsters for each of their voting citizens?
choose your adventure

this explains the problem. even with complete us military dominance over iran, iran can continue to disrupt the strait of hormuz and, with it, the global economy.


The White House (@WhiteHouse)

Iran’s ballistic missile capacity is functionally destroyed. Their navy assessed combat ineffective. Complete and total aerial dominance over Iran. 

Operation Epic Fury is yielding massive results. 🇺🇸





— https://nitter.net/WhiteHouse/status/2032833890168008832#m
sometimes followers send me things that make me chuckle
whether building critical minerals supply chains or securing the strait of hormuz, turns out the united states still really needs friends and allies.

“the days in which the middle east dominated american foreign policy in both long-term planning and day to day execution are thankfully over—not because the middle east no longer matters, but because it is no longer the constant irritant, and potential source of imminent catastrophe, that it once was.

conflict remains the middle east’s most troublesome dynamic, but there is today less to this problem than headlines might lead one to believe. iran—the region’s chief destabilizing force—has been

trump, last saturday: don’t need british ships. we will remember, we don’t need people that join wars after we’ve already won.

trump, this saturday: hopefully united kingdom will send ships so that strait of hormuz will no longer be threatened.
challenge for president trump: no matter how defeated the iranians are they can still threaten the global economy’s most important waterway. 🤔

meanwhile, events escalating in cuba. protestors set fire to a local communist party office…


Nora Gámez Torres (@ngameztorres)

Cubans in Morón, central Cuba, took to the streets banging pots and pans Friday night chanting “Down with communism” as some of the protesters broke into the local Communist Party building and tried to set it on fire. One person appeared to have been shot.
miamiherald.com/news/nation-…




— https://nitter.net/ngameztorres/status/2032787441799586153#m

“if not regime change, what then? the answer may be staring at us from a small island in the northern persian gulf. kharg island.”

gzeromedia.com/by-ian-bremme…
americans do not want—and did not vote for—boots on the ground in a war with iran.

trump didn’t fail to sell war with iran to americans and congress.
he chose not to.

Video

two weeks before us marine expeditionary force—some 2,500 marines—now being deployed to iran arrives in the mideast. 

this war (and global economic disruption) isn’t over soon.

wednesday: president trump tells g7 leaders iran is about to surrender, war is nearly over. 

friday:
americans (correctly) believe that tariffs have led to higher prices.

new (trump approved) supreme leader just dropped…

Video
staggering number here

cuba is talking to trump because it’s running out of oil, power, and time. trump bets he can recreate venezuela in cuba.
that's a better bet than iran.

this is true. strait is open and iranian tankers are transiting with oil bound for china. the united states is not interfering with iranian tanker traffic. 

there does remain the small matter of iran targeting other countries’ tankers...


OSINTdefender (@sentdefender)

U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth states that the Strait of Hormuz is “open for transit” and the only thing preventing that is continued drone and missile attacks by Iran.

i’m starting to get the feeling that the iranians are not about to surrender unconditionally.
